perturbation.original_text: The Parties acknowledge that each owns certain CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, as defined herein, which might relate to the inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how, discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA; and
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties acknowledge that each *may* own certain CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, as defined herein, which might relate to the inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how, discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA; and
perturbation.explanation: The use of 'may' instead of 'owns' introduces uncertainty about the existing ownership of confidential information. The original text clearly states that each party owns certain confidential information. By changing this to 'may,' the agreement becomes ambiguous about whether either party actually possesses such information, creating potential disputes over what constitutes confidential information and what obligations apply. This has an impact on the validity of the following clauses where the word information is used, contradicting the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge that each owns certain CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, as defined herein, which might relate to the inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how, discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA; and
perturbation.justification: The original text asserts that each party 'owns' certain confidential information. The changed text modifies this to 'may' own. This change introduces ambiguity and contradicts the original assertion of ownership. The contradicted text reinforces the original assertion that each party 'owns' confidential information. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, making it unclear whether either party actually possesses confidential information, hence creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement *may* mean some technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'means all' to 'may mean some', the definition of confidential information becomes unclear. Previously, any identified technical or business information was classified as confidential information, Now, the change introduces uncertainty, making it difficult to determine what qualifies as confidential information, because not all are considered, contradicting the other parts of the text where the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is refferred, and the meaning is ambiguous
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' means all technical or business information disclosed and identified within 30 days. The changed text alters this to 'may mean some', introducing ambiguity. This contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly defines 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' as 'all' information meeting the criteria. The change creates a direct conflict by making the definition of confidential information uncertain, whereas previously it was definitive. This uncertainty directly undermines the original intention and enforceability, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Any Party may also cause such disclosures to be made to the other Party on behalf of the disclosing Party by third parties who are Under obligations of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; such disclosures from third parties shall be deemed to be disclosures by the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Any Party may also cause such disclosures to be made to the other Party on behalf of the disclosing Party by third parties *regardless of* obligations of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; such disclosures from third parties shall be deemed to be disclosures by the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly alters the confidentiality requirements. Originally, disclosures by third parties were valid only if those third parties were under confidentiality obligations. Now, disclosures are considered valid even if the third parties are not bound by confidentiality, directly contradicting the purpose of the Non-Disclosure Agreement and introducing a risk of unauthorized disclosure. It affects the initial premise of the agreement and the responsibilities involved. Thus, the text is contradicting itself due to the inserted perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any Party may also cause such disclosures to be made to the other Party on behalf of the disclosing Party by third parties who are Under obligations of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; such disclosures from third parties shall be deemed to be disclosures by the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for third parties to be under confidentiality obligations when making disclosures. The contradicted text explicitly states that third parties *must* be under such obligations. This creates a direct conflict, as disclosures are now valid even without the necessary confidentiality, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' since key obligations are inconsistently defined.

perturbation.original_text: 4. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party and, upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain *sometimes* the property of the disclosing Party and, upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall *endeavor to* return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change compromises the clear ownership and return obligations for confidential information. The insertion of 'sometimes' before 'the property' creates ambiguity about who owns the information. Changing 'shall promptly return' to 'shall endeavor to return' weakens the obligation to return the information, making it optional. This change introduces an **internal contradiction** to what entails the 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party and, upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity ('sometimes' before 'the property') and weakens the obligation ('endeavor to' instead of 'shall promptly return') regarding confidential information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party' and 'the receiving Party shall promptly return', creating a clear inconsistency and making the obligations unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
